key_insight: PNOT reconstructs divertor temperature fields in real time by combining graph-based boundary physics with physics-aware neural operator attention and derivative-consistent training.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more generalizable temperature prediction for fusion divertors, supporting online monitoring and control to reduce melting risk and extend component life.
method: Models boundary heat-flux relations as a structured graph.
Uses graph attention to capture spatial physical dependencies.
Adds physics-aware neural operator slicing plus Sobolev regularization to enforce smooth, physically consistent fields.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental results showing improved prediction accuracy while preserving physical consistency on the EAST tungsten monoblock divertor temperature-field reconstruction task.
watch_item: Performance may depend on carefully defining the physical graph, slicing strategy, and derivative-based loss so the model remains stable across unseen operating conditions.
paper_title: Temperature Field Reconstruction of Tungsten Monoblock Divertor on EAST using Physics-aware Neural Operator Transformer
